changes: Fixed canonical memory views so only server-confirmed Short-term and Long-term memories receive lifecycle badges and filters
Fixed chat hanging on a spinning cursor when the model provider stalled mid-answer — the turn now ends with a clear error instead of waiting
Kept push-to-talk interruptions and background recovery visually minimal while preserving clear error messages
Fixed push-to-talk occasionally reporting a background agent as finished before its task had actually completed
Fixed the floating bar failing to escalate a hard voice question to the higher model at the start of a new conversation
Fixed push-to-talk screen questions so Omi answers the original question from a fresh, turn-specific screen capture instead of only reading back a visual report.
Fixed switching accounts showing the previous account's conversations and memories until the app was restarted
Fixed an open conversation and the folder filters still showing the previous account's data after switching accounts
Fixed folders and people from the previous account briefly reappearing right after switching accounts
Fixed an active conversations search still showing the previous account's results after switching accounts
Fixed Desktop agent SQL queries with names, dates, and search patterns so they no longer fail on malformed literals
The floating bar and notch island now stay above other overlay apps (e.g. Clicky) instead of being buried under them
Fixed background-agent completion states and results staying out of sync between push-to-talk and the agent list
Made push-to-talk keep one captured turn through a stale realtime session handoff or a fast transcript fallback instead of requiring a repeat press
Fixed voice input attempting to use a connection that had already closed
Fixed Rewind capture getting stuck after a recoverable local database failure instead of reopening its database automatically
Improved macOS Settings controls with better alignment, larger inputs, and more responsive interactions
Fixed task prioritization syncing large score updates by sending them in backend-safe batches
Improved macOS development bundle isolation and troubleshooting.
